verticals.description: Two-source rule, named sources, tensions block publication.

steps.title: Create an investigation
steps.body: Create an investigation for your story or fact-check run. Use a clear title (e.g. "Q1 revenue claim").

steps.title: Add evidence
steps.body: Ingest primary documents, witness statements, official records. Each item becomes evidence you can link to claims.

steps.title: Propose claims
steps.body: Add claims (assertions you want to defend). Start as working hypotheses; type and scope can be refined at Forge/Vault.

steps.title: Link support and challenge
steps.body: Link evidence spans to claims as support or challenge. Use the Reading flow: open evidence, pick span (or whole doc), then "Support claim X" or "Challenge claim Y".

steps.title: Review tension suggestions
steps.body: If the system suggests tensions between claims (e.g. contradiction), confirm or dismiss. Open tensions block Vault publication under the journalism policy.

steps.title: Advance tier and export
steps.body: Move to Forge when structure is ready; to Vault when ready to publish. Export .chronicle for verification or the submission package (reasoning briefs + manifest) for handoff.

verticals.description: Stricter MES, chain of custody, tension resolution for checkpoints.

steps.title: Create investigation
steps.body: One investigation per matter or brief. Set tier when appropriate (Spark for draft, Forge for structured, Vault for checkpoint).

steps.title: Ingest evidence
steps.body: Add primary documents and official records. Register sources when required by policy (Forge/Vault).

steps.title: Propose claims
steps.body: Claims map to legal assertions. Link support and challenge with rationale (warrant).

steps.title: Resolve tensions
steps.body: Address all tension suggestions: confirm (declare tension) or dismiss with rationale. Legal policy typically requires tension resolution before checkpoint.

steps.title: Chain of custody
steps.body: Under the legal profile, checkpoints may require chain-of-custody. Use export and reasoning briefs for audit.

steps.title: Export submission package
steps.body: Download the submission package (ZIP: .chronicle + reasoning_briefs/ + manifest) for filing or handoff. Verify the .chronicle with chronicle-verify.

verticals.description: Evidence admissibility, exception workflow, all tensions addressed.

steps.title: Create investigation
steps.body: One investigation per audit or compliance run. Use a descriptive title and optional investigation_key for idempotency.

steps.title: Add evidence
steps.body: Ingest evidence; prefer official records and primary documents. Policy may warn on one-sided claims (single source, no challenges).

steps.title: Propose claims
steps.body: Claims represent assertions under review. Link support and challenge; document rationale.

steps.title: Address tensions
steps.body: Review tension suggestions. Confirm or dismiss. Compliance profile may allow DEFERRED with justification; all tensions should be addressed before checkpoint.

steps.title: Exception workflow
steps.body: If using exception workflow (due dates, assigned_to), track overdue tensions and remediation type.

steps.title: Export and verify
steps.body: Export .chronicle and/or submission package. Verify with chronicle-verify before archiving or sharing.
